summary: Rectangular ceremonial or temple-like structure with a central raised tiled roof spine and four tall corner towers connected by low striped walls. A raised walkway of red brick tiles runs down the center between towers; towers capped with wide overhanging flat tops with stone trim and sea-lantern centers. Exterior walls show alternating horizontal light/darker bands and the whole sits on a low white-block base ring on a packed-earth plot.

shape.overall_form: long rectangle with four corner towers and a raised central roof ridge
shape.symmetry: bilaterally symmetrical along the long axis (approximate)

materials.name: brick
materials.role: central walkway and tiled roof sections

materials.name: quartz
materials.role: white base blocks and vertical entrance/trim

materials.name: sea_lantern
materials.role: light source at tower caps and central walkway

elements: four tall corner towers with overhanging caps
red brick central walkway with sea-lanterns
small square windows or iron trap-style vents on towers
rebuild_hints: Place a rectangular footprint roughly 11x25 blocks on packed-earth; add a one-block high white base plinth around perimeter using quartz (or white block) with 1-block gaps matching seen pattern.

Build four identical towers near each corner, ~3-4 blocks thick, ~12-14 blocks tall; add small square recessed windows evenly spaced vertically; cap each tower with an overhanging slab/trim layer and a sea-lantern centered on the cap.

Create a central raised ridge of red brick stairs/tiles between the two inner towers forming a stepped roof; align a 3-block wide red brick walkway on top connecting towers, with sea-lantern in center line.
Use alternating horizontal bands of lighter and slightly darker sandstone (or sand-colored blocks) for the long side walls to reproduce the striped pattern.
Add decorative small stone or slab blocks under tower caps and at base corners to match visible cornice details.
uncertainties: Exact block types for light beige stripes: appears to be sandstone and smooth_sandstone but could be different sand-colored blocks.
Tower face material: looks like sandstone with some stonebrick/diorite trim — identification uncertain between stonebrick, smoothstone, or diorite for vertical corner trim.
Exact footprint dimensions and internal layout unseen; width/depth/height are approximate based on external views and may vary by a few blocks.
Some decorative small blocks (possible buttons, iron trapdoors or windows) on tower faces are unclear; recreated as small square vents/windows.
